Class OutboxRelayScheduler

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.out.messaging.OutboxRelayScheduler
@Component public class OutboxRelayScheduler extends Object
Scheduled background component that implements the relay half of the Transactional Outbox Pattern.

On each scheduling cycle (default: every 5 seconds), this component:

  1. Queries the outbox_messages table for all unpublished records.
  2. Publishes each record's payload to RabbitMQ via MessagePublisherPort.
  3. Marks successfully delivered records as published and persists the change.

If RabbitMQ is unavailable or rejects a message, the exception is caught and logged. The record remains published=false and will be retried on the next cycle, providing at-least-once delivery semantics.

Each outbox message is processed in its own independent transaction to prevent a single failing message from blocking all other pending messages.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Constructor Details

    • OutboxRelayScheduler

      public OutboxRelayScheduler(OutboxMessageRepository outboxMessageRepository, MessagePublisherPort messagePublisherPort, io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry meterRegistry)
      Constructs an OutboxRelayScheduler with its required dependencies.
      Parameters:
      outboxMessageRepository - the repository for querying and updating outbox records
      messagePublisherPort - the adapter that publishes messages to RabbitMQ
      meterRegistry - the Micrometer registry for relay metrics

  • Method Details

    • relay

      @Scheduled(fixedDelayString="${banking.outbox.relay.fixed-delay-ms:5000}") public void relay()
      Polls for unpublished outbox messages and relays them to RabbitMQ.

      Runs on a fixed delay defined by the banking.outbox.relay.fixed-delay-ms property (default: 5000 ms). Each message is processed in its own Transactional boundary via relayMessage(OutboxMessage).

    • relayMessage

      @Transactional public void relayMessage(OutboxMessage message)
      Publishes a single outbox message and marks it as delivered.

      This method runs in its own transaction so that a failure on one message does not prevent others from being processed.

      Parameters:
      message - the outbox message to relay
